revision_summary: Stage 3 requires one targeted rerun addressing the following: (1) BUTTON_UP GAP — The analysis entirely omits evaluation of the button_up required elements. Four of five codex-required elements (one_hour_block_stated, phone_appointment_specified, no_driving_at_computer, is_that_fair_handshake) are absent from the transcript. The codex explicitly ties no_driving_at_computer and is_that_fair_handshake to no-show rate. These must appear as cherry_pick_moments and in coaching_focus. The handoff_brief should note show-rate risk from missing no_driving_at_computer anchor. (2) BILL ANCHOR SEVERITY — coaching_focus[1] must be recalibrated upward. No bill was captured at any point in this call. The codex's coaching_philosophy.bill_anchoring section should be explicitly cited, and the framing should reflect a codex-graded gap ('universal coaching priority') not 'slightly harder for the closer.' (3) IRON SOLAR AMBIGUITY — The handoff_brief must flag 'Iron Solar' vs 'ION Solar' as an explicit closer action item: if Iron Solar is a competitor (likely), the closer should know Juan is competitor-pitched and may be comparing proposals. (4) TIME-SLOT AMBIGUITY — The calendar-gap analysis at 02:41 must note that the confirmed time is '10AM' per Randy but the customer said '10:10' — the confirmation text should lock '10AM' to avoid arrival-time confusion. (5) HYPOTHESIS FLIP — cherry_pick_moments[1] alternative_hypothesis should be corrected: the 'strategic bypass' read is weaker than Stage 3 presents given the absence of any bill capture throughout the entire call. The reactive read should be the primary hypothesis. (6) MISSING LEGITIMACY MOMENT — Juan's confirmation at 00:29 ('That was for solar. I did do some type of estimate last week') should be added as a cherry_pick moment — it is the moment Randy's legitimacy anchor lands and is a floor-teachable signal.
